rationale: No DAG/merge-signal regression in the 943cc310 delta. (a) My round-1 items applied correctly: B4's key_file_paths now carry cmd/mindspec/root.go + help_golden_test.go, and B4's verification re-runs TestPanelSchemaDoc_MatchesConstants after its interfaces.md append. (b) The B3/B4 'shared' fixture creates no coupling and no new edge: the plan is explicit that the tables are NOT import-shared — B3 holds PanelStateEntry-shaped rows, B4 holds GateFacts rows, same branch coverage, with a justified asymmetry (transient-GitErr is B4-only because BranchSHAResolver's (sha, bool) return has no error state — verified against internal/instruct). B3 stays a root; B4 still depends only on B1. (c) Frontmatter depends_on is byte-identical to round 1; no new verification step inverts bead order (B2's branch-built binary needs no other bead; the B1-stub/B5-skill 'Your job' handoff rides the existing 5→4→1 chain). One wording nit, non-blocking, in findings.

findings.title: Round-1 F2 findings ADDRESSED
findings.detail: B4 key_file_paths += cmd/mindspec/root.go, cmd/mindspec/help_golden_test.go (frontmatter at 943cc310, verified). B4 verification now re-runs `go test ./internal/panel -run 'TestPanelSchemaDoc_MatchesConstants$'` after the doc-sync append, with the correct rationale (additive edit to the same file B1's test reads). Both applied as suggested.

findings.title: No new cross-bead coupling from the branch-complete fixture tables
findings.detail: The BRIEF summary's phrase 'shared fixture with B3' overstates; the plan text is the precise one — 'not literally import-shared across the two packages'. Each bead owns its own table over the same gate.go branch rows, so neither bead compiles against the other's test code, B3 remains a root, and B4's only edge is still B1. The one asymmetry (GitErr row present only in B4's table) is real, not an omission: internal/instruct builds facts from BranchSHAResolver's (sha string, exists bool) return, which cannot express a transient git error; only the GateIO.RevParse seam can. Duplicated safely.

findings.title: One inaccurate clause in B3 step 3's rationale — mechanics still sound
findings.detail: The plan says the four deleted message literals 'live solely in gate.go's Decision.Message and are relayed'. True for three; false for the fourth: "REJECT or hard_block is recorded" exists only at internal/instruct/panelstate.go:146 — gate.go:251 words that branch "HARD block / REJECT recorded — halt path, see /ms-panel-tally". The source-text absence assertion is still valid (the literal is unique to the doomed local branch), and the existing TestPanelStateEntry_Verdict rows pin only the substring "halt path", which gate.go's message also contains, so 'keep existing tests green' holds. Rendered wording for that one case changes slightly at runtime; nothing pins it. Cosmetic plan-prose fix at most; no change required.

findings.title: DAG edges and merge story otherwise untouched
findings.detail: depends_on identical to round 1 (roots 1/2/3; 4<-1; 5<-4). New gates respect the order: B2's self-check now builds /tmp/ms110b2 from the branch (no dependency on B1/B3/B4 — their files are additive and absent at B2 time, the package compiles); B1's new machine-managed 'Your job' stub and B5's removal of the skill-authored duplicate are ordered by the existing 5->4->1 chain (the brief two-bead window where both blocks could coexist requires manually invoking the not-yet-skill-wired verb — transient, G3's accepted territory). panel.VerdictApprove/VerdictRequestChanges/VerdictReject constants referenced by B1's payload pins exist today (internal/panel/tally.go:19-21). §Risks/112 text unchanged.
